title: Google's profits are outrunning its AI spending boom: AlphaCheck
excerpt: Alphabet's AI spending has surged — so have sales and profits. The company has made the balancing act look almost easy.
content_html:
Alphabet's (GOOGL) AI spending has surged. So have sales and profits.

Earnings after the bell will show whether that unlikely combination can hold.

So far, Alphabet has made the balancing act look almost easy.

Its operating margin — the share of sales left after running the business — reached 36.1% in the first quarter, its highest level in five years. Revenue grew 22%, while operating income jumped 30%.

Growth has helped do the heavy lifting.

Google Cloud revenue surged 63% last quarter to more than $20 billion, while Search revenue rose 19%. Cloud operating income tripled, giving Alphabet a rapidly expanding profit engine alongside its advertising business.

Now the bill is getting much bigger.

Alphabet spent $35.7 billion last quarter on servers, data centers, and other infrastructure and equipment. Wall Street expects quarterly capital expenditures, or capex, to approach $44 billion in Wednesday's report, while the company plans to spend as much as $190 billion for the full year.

It has already warned that 2027 spending will be "significantly" higher.

The cash goes out immediately, but the hit to profits arrives over time.

Once new servers and data centers enter service, Alphabet gradually records their cost through depreciation. Alphabet generally spreads the cost of servers and network equipment across six years, meaning today's spending can weigh on profits well into the future.

That delayed bill is already growing. Alphabet said depreciation would accelerate this year as earlier investments entered service, adding to energy and other data center costs.

Wall Street expects Alphabet's operating margin to ease to 34.7% this quarter as spending approaches $44 billion.

Holding margins near 35% while sales keep growing around 20% would show Alphabet is still outrunning its AI bill. A much weaker margin as spending climbs would show the bill is catching up.

Jared Blikre is the global markets and data editor for Yahoo Finance. Follow him on X at @SPYJared or email him at jaredblikre@yahooinc.com.

content_html_zh:
Alphabet (GOOGL) 的人工智能支出激增。 销售额和利润也是如此。

盘后的收益将显示这种不太可能的组合是否能够维持。

到目前为止，Alphabet 已经让这种平衡行为看起来几乎很容易。

第一季度其营业利润率（运营业务后剩余的销售额份额）达到 36.1%，为五年来的最高水平。 收入增长 22%，营业收入增长 30%。

增长帮助完成了繁重的任务。

上季度谷歌云收入飙升 63%，超过 200 亿美元，而搜索收入增长 19%。 云运营收入增长了两倍，为 Alphabet 及其广告业务提供了快速扩张的利润引擎。

现在账单变得越来越大。

Alphabet 上季度在服务器、数据中心以及其他基础设施和设备上花费了 357 亿美元。 华尔街在周三的报告中预计季度资本支出（资本支出）将接近 440 亿美元，而该公司计划全年支出高达 1900 亿美元。

它已经警告称，2027 年的支出将“显着”增加。

现金会立即花掉，但利润会随着时间的推移而受到影响。

一旦新的服务器和数据中心投入使用，Alphabet 就会逐渐通过折旧记录其成本。 Alphabet 通常将服务器和网络设备的成本分摊到六年内，这意味着今天的支出可能会影响未来的利润。

延迟的账单已经在增加。 Alphabet 表示，随着早期投资投入使用，今年的折旧将会加速，从而增加能源和其他数据中心成本。

华尔街预计，随着支出接近 440 亿美元，Alphabet 本季度的营业利润率将降至 34.7%。

将利润率保持在 35% 左右，而销售额保持在 20% 左右增长，这表明 Alphabet 仍然超出了其 AI 成本。 随着支出攀升，利润率大幅下降将表明该法案正在迎头赶上。

Jared Blikre 是雅虎财经的全球市场和数据编辑。 在 X 上关注他 @SPYJared 或发送电子邮件至 jaredblikre@yahooinc.com。
